message:
fix(delta-parser): add JSON sanitization for common LLM errors

Problem
-------
LLM-generated JSON often contains syntax errors that cause parse failures:
- Trailing commas after the last element in arrays/objects
- Single-line comments (// ...) explaining values
- Multi-line comments (/* ... */) for documentation

These are valid in JavaScript but invalid in strict JSON, and LLMs
frequently produce them when outputting structured data.

Solution
--------
Add sanitizeJson() preprocessing step that cleans common errors before
parsing. If standard JSON.parse() fails, the sanitized version is tried.

The sanitization:
1. Removes single-line comments (//) while preserving string literals
2. Removes multi-line comments (/* */) while preserving string literals
3. Removes trailing commas before closing braces/brackets

The regex-based approach preserves quoted strings containing comment-like
sequences (e.g., "url: https://example.com") while stripping actual comments.

Implementation
--------------
```typescript
function sanitizeJson(str: string): string {
  // Match: "strings" OR // comments OR /* comments */
  const commentRegex = /("(?:[^"\\]|\\.)*")|(\/\/.*)|(\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\/)/g;

  let cleaned = str.replace(commentRegex, (match, strGroup) => {
    if (strGroup) return match; // Keep string literal
    return ""; // Remove comment
  });

  // Remove trailing commas
  cleaned = cleaned.replace(/,(\s*[}\]])/g, "$1");
  return cleaned;
}
```
